name: Melanopareiidae
description: Crescentchests are small, long-tailed passerines of South American dry forests and scrublands. They are named for the distinctive crescent-shaped breast band that most species possess.

children.name: Melanopareia
children.description: Melanopareia is the sole genus in the family, containing five species found across western and central South America.

children.name: Melanopareia torquata
children.description: The collared crescentchest has a bold black breast band and rufous crown, inhabiting the Cerrado savannas of central Brazil. Its long tail is often cocked upward as it forages.

children.name: Melanopareia maximiliani
children.description: The olive-crowned crescentchest is found in dry scrub and forest edges of the Gran Chaco region. It has an olive crown and a narrow black crescent across the chest.

children.name: Melanopareia elegans
children.description: The elegant crescentchest has striking rufous underparts and a black crescent on the upper breast. It is endemic to the Tumbesian region of southwestern Ecuador and northwestern Peru.
